frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Computationally cheap inaccurate system giving rise to habits.

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Computationally expensive accurate system implementing planning.

frameworks.name: cost-benefit arbitration
frameworks.one_line: Online comparison of each system's task-specific costs and benefits.

core_question: How do people allocate control between habitual model-free and planned model-based reinforcement-learning systems?
key_assumptions: Habit vs planning maps onto model-free vs model-based RL competition
Each system carries task-specific costs and benefits comparable online
Allocation can flexibly shift with task demands
manipulations: Relative accuracy of model-based vs model-free control across tasks
Reward magnitude amplifying benefits of accurate performance
key_findings: Model-based control increases when it yields greater accuracy than model-free control
Reward amplification further boosts model-based control when accuracy differs
Reward amplification has no effect when the two systems yield equivalent accuracy
interpretations: Humans adaptively balance habit and planning via online cost-benefit analysis
Arbitration reflects sensitivity to task-specific net utility of each system
